name: Sierra de San Pedro Circuit
name_original: La Roca de la Sierra desde Aliseda – circular por la Sierra de San Pedro y el Embalse de Villar del Rey
description: This difficult 66.8-mile road cycling route from Aliseda offers a challenging circular tour through the Sierra de San Pedro and Embalse de V
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Aliseda traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and open countryside in the Extremadura region of Spain. The area features natural trails and routes that often pass by reservoirs, such as Embalse Molano, and through agricultural lands. Elevations are generally moderate, with some routes featuring more significant climbs, particularly towards areas like the Sierra de San Pedro. The terrain offers a mix of flat sections and undulating roads suitable for various road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Aliseda The most popular road cycling route is Cáceres-Badajoz Corridor Natural Trail Loop, a 56.6 miles (91.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a natural corridor, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de San Pedro Circuit, a difficult 66.7 miles (107.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de San Pedro, providing a challenging ride. Local road cyclists also love the Embalse Molano loop from Arroyo de la Luz, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Road cycling around Aliseda is defined by open countryside, reservoir views, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy road bike trails Aliseda to challenging road bike routes Aliseda. The routes in Aliseda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aliseda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Aliseda?
A: There are currently 8 road cycling routes available around Aliseda on komoot, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Aliseda?
A: Yes, Aliseda offers easy road cycling routes. One option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40321605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Molano loop from Arroyo de la Luz&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.5 miles (47.5 km) long and features moderate elevation changes, making it a good choice for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Aliseda area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057829005/sierra-de-san-pedro-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Pedro Circuit&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over its 66.7 miles (107.4 km). Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5304706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square of Brozas – Malpartida de Cáceres loop from Cáceres&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 72.5 miles (116.7 km) with substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes around Aliseda?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057829005/sierra-de-san-pedro-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Pedro Circuit&lt;/a&gt; covers 66.7 miles (107.4 km). Another excellent long-distance option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5304706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square of Brozas – Malpartida de Cáceres loop from Cáceres&lt;/a&gt;, which extends to 72.5 miles (116.7 km).

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes near Aliseda?
A: Road cycling routes around Aliseda traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and open countryside in the Extremadura region of Spain. You'll often pass by reservoirs, such as Embalse Molano, and through agricultural lands, offering varied and scenic views.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Aliseda?
A: Many of the routes around Aliseda are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057886509/caceres-badajoz-corridor-natural-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cáceres-Badajoz Corridor Natural Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40321605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Molano loop from Arroyo de la Luz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you might encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aceitunilla Pond&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5579441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmites of the Salor River&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5218841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge Over the Salor River (Brozas Road)&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable point of interest.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Aliseda?
A: The Extremadura region generally experiences hot summers. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, with milder weather and less intense sun, making for more comfortable rides.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Aliseda?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from open countryside to reservoir views, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there routes with significant elevation changes for climbing practice?
A: Yes, routes heading towards the Sierra de San Pedro offer more significant climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057829005/sierra-de-san-pedro-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Pedro Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, providing a challenging workout for climbers.

Q: Where can I find parking for road biking near Aliseda?
A: Many routes, particularly those starting from towns like Arroyo de la Luz or Cáceres, offer convenient parking options in or near the town centers. For specific trailheads, checking the individual route details on komoot will provide the most accurate parking information.
tips: Spectacular views of the surrounding landscape from Puerto del Zángano. With a length of 2 km and a gradient of 3.5%, this lcimb is not too difficult.

La Roca de la Sierra is a municipality in the province of Badajoz, located between Puebla de Obando and Villar del Rey. In the municipality there is a set of hills to the northwest of the town called Las Sierrillas.

Reservoir that is located in the west of the province of Badajoz and that collects mainly the waters of the Sierra de San Pedro that is located in its north. It is located close to the center of Villar del Rey, which gives it its name.

There have been several archaeological finds that indicate an origin of Aliseda much earlier than the 13th century. The actual date of the city's founding is unknown, but the area has been used in some form since before Christ.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, be prepared for a challenging experience. The terrain includes paved roads but also segments that may be unpaved and difficult for traditional road bikes, potentially requiring you to dismount and push. The route traverses the Sierra de San Pedro, characterized by modest but steep relief, with quartzite rocky alignments and slate valleys. You'll pass through 'dehesas' with holm oaks and cork oaks.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires a very good level of fitness. Its length, significant elevation changes, and potential for unpaved sections make it challenging for road cyclists, and it is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers stunning scenery through the Sierra de San Pedro. You'll pass through the settlement of Aliseda and the village of La Roca de la Sierra. A notable point is the Puerto de El Zángano, and you'll enjoy beautiful View of Villar del Rey Reservoir. The landscape is dominated by 'dehesas' of holm and cork oaks, typical of Extremadura.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Sierra de San Pedro?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de San Pedro is a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), so it's rich in avian wildlife. Keep an eye out for species such as the Iberian imperial eagle, black vulture, black stork, and golden eagle. The 'dehesas' also support diverse ecosystems.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Aliseda. You should be able to find parking within the town to begin your circular journey. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but as a starting point, Aliseda offers access.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Sierra de San Pedro?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de San Pedro is a protected natural area. While there are no general entrance fees for cycling, it's always advisable to respect local regulations for protected zones. No specific cycling permits are mentioned as required for this route, but always stay on marked paths and respect the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, it is primarily a challenging road cycling route. Bringing dogs on such a long and demanding ride, especially with potential unpaved sections, might not be ideal for their safety or comfort. If you do bring a dog, ensure they are well-conditioned for long distances and always kept on a leash, especially in protected wildlife areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route would generally be during the spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November). These seasons offer milder temperatures, which are more suitable for a long and difficult ride. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, and winters can be cooler and wetter, making the unpaved sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through settlements like Aliseda and La Roca de la Sierra. These villages, and others in the broader Sierra de San Pedro region, typically offer local cafes, restaurants, and guesthouses where you can find refreshments or accommodation. It's recommended to check availability in advance, especially in smaller towns.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Camino Natural Corredor Cáceres-Badajoz. Ruta Cáceres-Badajoz-Embalse de Piedra Aguda, the Camino Natural del Corredor Cáceres - Badajoz, and the Cañada de Sancha Brava, among others.

faqs.question: What should I know about the mixed terrain if I'm cycling on a racebike?
faqs.answer: If you're on a racebike, be aware that while much of the route is paved, some segments are unpaved and can be quite difficult. You might need to dismount and push your bike through these sections. For a smoother experience on varied surfaces, a gravel bike or mountain bike might be more suitable for certain parts of this challenging route.
